reasoning: FIDE updates official ratings on the 1st of every month, and the Wikipedia page mirrors this monthly release cycle. On Jan 25, 2026, the page will almost certainly display the exact same January 2026 list as it did on Jan 18. This means his Elo will be 2734 on both dates — a 0% change, far below the required +1% (which would be an increase of >27 points to ~2762). For the Feb 17 resolution date, the February FIDE list will apply. However, a 27+ point Elo gain in a single month is extremely rare for a super GM rated >2700, as it requires dominating a long, elite classical tournament. MVL is not listed as participating in January's major classical tournament (Tata Steel Masters), and classical activity at his level in January outside of Tata Steel typically doesn't offer enough volume to gain 27 points, let alone without drawing/losing. Therefore, the chance of checking a 1% higher rating on either date is basically the minimum allowable probability. I am setting it slightly higher for the second date to conform to the instruction that distant horizons should have probabilities closer to 0.5.
